pages:
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLCwww.saltmarshenv.com
April 30, 2016
Michelle Roberts
Chief Financial Officer
Dyer and Sons
Re: Phase I Environmental Site Assessment, Dyer and Sons
Dear Ms. Roberts:
This letter sets out the terms on which this firm will conduct a Phase I
Environmental Site Assessment of the subject property, and asks you to countersign
it so the work can be put in the schedule. We expect the assessment to run about
four months from commencement to submission of the final report. No field work
has been performed, and none will be until a countersigned copy of this letter is
back in our hands.
Scope and Approach
The assessment will be performed in general conformance with ASTM E1527-13 and
the All Appropriate Inquiries rule at 40 CFR Part 312. In practice that means four
things, in order. Standard historical sources will be reviewed, including aerial
photography, fire insurance maps, the topographic series, and such county records
as exist on fill, grading, and shoreline work. Regulatory database records will be
obtained for the property and for the search radii the standard prescribes. A site
reconnaissance will be conducted covering the property, and the adjoining
properties as they can be observed from the property and from public rights of way.
Interviews will be conducted with persons reasonably likely to have knowledge of
the property's past use.
The estuarine setting adds a step we do not skip. Tidal influence, fill history, and
past shoreline work are exactly the things the standard sources record poorly, and
on a parcel of this kind they are usually where the answer lives. The reconnaissance
will therefore be scheduled against the tide, so that the water edge of the parcel is
walked rather than described from forty feet away at high water.
The deliverable is a written report identifying recognized environmental conditions,
controlled recognized environmental conditions, and historical recognized
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 1 of 4

environmental conditions as those terms are defined in the standard, together with
the data gaps encountered and our opinion of whether they are significant. Where
the record does not support a conclusion, the report will say so in those words. That
is a professional obligation rather than a hedge for its own sake, and I would rather
write down what is not known than let a reader assume it was checked. A draft will
be issued for your review, and one round of comment is included before the final is
submitted.
The following are outside this scope: sampling of soil, sediment, groundwater, or
building materials; any wetland delineation; and any Phase II work. If the
assessment turns up a condition that warrants sampling, we will describe the
condition, stop, and quote the additional work separately rather than proceed on our
own authority.
Project Team
I will serve as Managing Principal and will read the report before it leaves this
office, as I do with everything a lender or an agency will eventually see. John
Sanchez, our Senior Scientist, will lead the records review and the reconnaissance.
Mary Foster, Field Technician, will support the reconnaissance and keep the field
record, including conditions, weather, and tide state on each visit. James Henderson,
Environmental Scientist, will review the database findings and is the person to call if
the scope has to open into sampling. All of us are available to Dyer and Sons
through the course of the work; day to day, John Sanchez is the right first call.
Fee and Schedule
The work will be performed for a fixed fee of $34,000, covering the historical
records review, the database review, the site reconnaissance, the interviews, the
draft and final reports, and one round of comment on the draft. Work will commence
on May 10, 2016, subject to receipt of this letter countersigned and to site access.
From that date we expect roughly four months to the final submission. The schedule
assumes access to the property on reasonable notice and reasonable cooperation
from the parties we ask to interview. Delay in one or the other is the most common
reason a schedule of this kind slips, and I would rather say so now than explain it in
September.
Fees and Payment
Invoices will be issued monthly against progress and are payable within thirty days
of the invoice date. Out-of-pocket costs, including the database order and
reproduction of the historical sources, are included in the fixed fee stated above;
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 2 of 4

there is no separate expense line on this engagement. Work outside the scope
described above will not be performed without your written authorization and a
separately agreed fee. Amounts unpaid after sixty days may, at our option, suspend
further work, and you will have that in writing from me before it happens rather
than after.
Term and Termination
The engagement begins on the commencement date stated above and ends on
delivery of the final report, unless extended in writing. Either party may terminate
on ten days' written notice. On termination you will owe that portion of the fixed fee
corresponding to work performed through the termination date, and we will deliver
the work product in whatever condition it then exists, together with the field record.
Obligations that by their nature survive termination, including confidentiality and
payment for work performed, survive it.
Confidentiality
Information you provide, and the findings of the assessment, will be treated as
confidential and will not be released to a third party without your written consent,
except where disclosure is required by law or by an agency with jurisdiction. That
exception is not boilerplate. A condition carrying a statutory reporting duty will be
reported, and you will hear it from us before or at the time it goes to the agency,
never afterward. Our field notes and working files remain the property of this firm.
The report is yours, for the purpose for which it was prepared.
Limitation of Liability
A Phase I assessment is an inquiry into reasonably ascertainable information within
the scope of the standard. It is not a certification that the property is free of
contamination, and no such certification is offered or implied. Findings rest on
conditions as observed on the dates of the reconnaissance and on information
available when the report is issued; conditions change, and records surface late. To
the fullest extent permitted by law, the aggregate liability of this firm to Dyer and
Sons arising out of this engagement, whether in contract, tort, or otherwise, is
limited to the amount of the fee actually paid for the work. Reliance on the report is
limited to the client named above; extension of reliance to any other party requires
our written agreement.
Governing Law
This letter is governed by the laws of the state in which the subject property is
situated, without regard to its conflict of laws provisions, and any action arising
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 3 of 4

under it will be brought in the courts of that state. Before either of us files anything,
both of us agree to sit down once, in the same room, with the people who actually
did the work.
If the foregoing reflects your understanding, please sign both copies where
indicated and return one to my attention. Once it is back we will confirm the
reconnaissance dates with you and set them against the tide. Please call me directly
with any question on the scope or the terms; I would rather answer it now than have
it raised for the first time in the report.
Sincerely,
Angela Flynn
Managing Principal
Date: April 30, 2016
Michelle Roberts
Chief Financial Officer, Dyer and Sons
Date: April 30, 2016
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 4 of 4
